title: Examples and edge cases deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Examples and edge cases; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Examples and edge cases: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? One normal example is enough because boundary and invalid inputs are unlikely to occur in real use.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Examples should deliberately cover equivalence classes, boundaries, emptiness, duplication, sign, and invalid data that can change the required behavior.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Examples should deliberately cover equivalence classes, boundaries, emptiness, duplication, sign, and invalid data that can change the required behavior.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Examples and edge cases instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Examples should deliberately cover equivalence classes, boundaries, emptiness, duplication, sign, and invalid data that can change the required behavior.
One normal example is enough because boundary and invalid inputs are unlikely to occur in real use.

answer.explanation: Examples should deliberately cover equivalence classes, boundaries, emptiness, duplication, sign, and invalid data that can change the required behavior.
